text: STEM or STEAM education is one of the approaches to develop 21st-century skills. It fosters the creativity to develop the skills. This study uses systematic literature review method inspired by the work of Sharif and Moher et al. using PRISMA. The content analysis in this study aims to identify trends of STEM or STEAM education research in Indonesia by analyzing articles from both local and international journals. Total of 63 articles were collected based on the inclusion criteria and then analyzed using Paper Classification Form (PCF) instrument adapted from Kizilaslan et al. which has indicators of title, author, author nation, journal name, year of publication, journal type, language, index, main discipline, research method, data collection, sample, and data analysis. Based on the analysis, the dominating topic is learning strategies with experimental research method. STEM or STEAM education has been developed in secondary schools, especially for science subjects. The dominating learning models are project-based learning, problem-based learning, and Inquiry integrated with STEM or STEM learning. The dominating theme of critical thinking skill, scientific literacy, and learning outcomes are dominating in STEM or STEAM education. Further empirical research are needed to investigate the potentials of STEAM education to develop 21st-century learning skills.
